description: Phase 11+12+13+15 capability for the main + mascot windows — sidecar exec (wizard|session) + 3 URL opens + scoped fs + push-to-mute global shortcut + mascot window state/visibility/click-through + recording reveal/open-external. Tauri 2.x auto-allows webview→app-command invocation by default for the 14 app commands registered via invoke_handler in main.rs: forward_ipc_to_sidecar, restart_sidecar, read_first_run_state, write_first_run_state, read_mascot_window_state, write_mascot_window_state, set_mascot_visible, set_mascot_click_through, open_screen_recording_settings, open_microphone_settings, request_microphone_permission, rebind_hotkey, reveal_in_os, open_input_wav. The 'app:allow-<command>' identifier namespace is reserved for Tauri's built-in core-app plugin (allow-name/allow-version/etc), NOT user #[tauri::command] entries — enumerating them would fail the build with 'permission identifier not found'. Warning #4: window-picker enumeration goes over the WS bus via ipc.calibration.list_windows; NO window-enum Tauri command exists. Phase 13 Plan 02: mascot window label 'mascot' added to scope so the 4 mascot commands work against both windows. Phase 15 Plan 02: tauri.conf.json5 exposes the assetProtocol with scope $APPDATA/vibemix/recordings/** + $APPLOCALDATA/vibemix/recordings/** for the in-drawer recording browser's <audio> playback — plugin-less per Tauri 2 (no permission identifier required here; scope is enforced by the Tauri runtime against the asset:// URLs convertFileSrc() emits). Phase 15 Plan 03: shell:allow-open extended with $APPDATA/vibemix/recordings/**/* + $APPLOCALDATA/vibemix/recordings/**/* path-scope entries so the open_input_wav command (tauri-plugin-shell open() shell-out to the OS-default audio app) is permitted only on session-dir-scoped input.wav files. The reveal_in_os command does NOT use shell:allow-open (it shells out via std::process::Command directly to `open -R` / `explorer /select,`); its security gate is the Rust-side validate_under_root canonicalize-prefix check.
